title: Deploy PDC agent on a private network and test connection

blocks.content: In the final step of your journey, you start the PDC agent using the deployment method you chose in Grafana Cloud (standalone binary, Kubernetes, or Docker).
You'll bring the agent online in your environment, then use Grafana Cloud to confirm it connected.

If you deploy on Kubernetes or Docker, follow the instructions in Grafana Cloud for that method: use the published PDC agent image, supply your PDC signing token and any host or cluster values the UI shows, and start the workload. For more detail, see [Configure Private data source connect](/docs/grafana-cloud/connect-externally-hosted/private-data-source-connect/configure-pdc).

When the agent is running, continue to the connection test step at the end of this section.

blocks.hint: Skip if you deployed on Kubernetes or Docker.
blocks.content:
If you installed the standalone binary on a server, on that machine open a terminal and go to the directory that contains the agent files. For example, after extracting the Linux x86_64 tarball:

```
cd pdc-agent_Linux_x86_64
```

blocks.hint: Skip if you deployed on Kubernetes or Docker.
blocks.content: If you are using the standalone binary, in Grafana Cloud copy the command in the **Run this binary** field.

blocks.hint: Skip if you deployed on Kubernetes or Docker.
blocks.content:
If you are using the standalone binary, in your server terminal run the command you copied.

You should receive a success message indicating that the PDC agent is running.

In Grafana Cloud, click **Test agent connection**.

You should receive an **Agent connected** message.
The agent is running on your network and Grafana Cloud confirmed the connection.
You can now install a data source plugin.

---

### Troubleshooting

Explore the following troubleshooting topics if you need help:

- [No such file or directory message appears](/docs/grafana-cloud/connect-externally-hosted/private-data-source-connect/configure-pdc#no-such-file-or-directory)
